author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XXVI. 26-28.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And as they were eating, Jesus took bread, and gave thanks, and brake it, and gaveit to the disciples, and said, Take, eat; This is my body.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And He took a cup, and gave thanks, and gave it to them, saying, Drink ye all of it; This is my blood of the New Testament, Which is shed for many, for the remission of sins.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Ah! how great is the blindness of the traitor!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Even partaking of the mysteries, he remained the same; and admitted to the most holytable, he changed not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this Luke shows by saying, that after this Satan enteredinto him, not as despising the Lord’s body, but thenceforth laughing to scorn the traitor’s shamelessness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed his sin became greater from both causes, as well in that he came to the mysteries with such a disposition, as that having approached them, he did not become better, either from fear, or from the benefit, or from the honor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But Christ forbad him not, although He knew all things, that thou mightest learn that He omits none of the things that pertain to correction.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore both before this, and after this, He continually admonished him, and checked him, both by deeds, and by words; both by fear, and by kindness; both by threatening, and by honor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But none of these things withdrew him from that grievous pest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore thenceforth He leaves him, and by the mysteries again reminds the disciples of His being slain, and in the midst of the meal His discourse is of the cross, by the continual repeating of the prediction, making His passion easy to receive.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if, when so many things had been done and foretold, they were troubled; if they had heard none of these things, what would they not have felt?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And as they were eating, He took bread, and brake it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why can it have been that He ordained this sacrament then, at the time of the passover?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That thou mightest learn from everything, both that He is the lawgiver of the Old Testament, and that the things therein are foreshadowed because of these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore, I say, where the type is, there He puts the truth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But the evening is a sure sign of the fullness of times, and that the things were now come to the very end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He gives thanks, to teach us how we ought to celebrate this sacrament, and to show that not unwillingly doth He come to the passion, and to teach us whatever we may suffer to bear it thankfully, thence also suggesting good hopes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if the type was a deliverance from such bondage, how much more will the truth set free the world, and will He be delivered up for the benefit of our race.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore, I would add, neither did He appoint the sacrament before this, but when henceforth the rites of the law were to cease.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And thus the very chief of the feasts He brings to an end, removing them to another most awful table, and He saith, “Take, eat, This is my body, Which is broken for many.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how were they not confounded at hearing this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because He had before told unto them many and great things touching this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore that He establishes no more, for they had heard it sufficiently, but he speaks of the cause of His passion, namely, the taking away of sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He calls it blood of a New Testament, that of the undertaking, the promise, the new law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this He undertook also of old, and this comprises the Testament that is in the new law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And like as the Old Testament had sheep and bullocks, so this has the Lord’s blood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hence also He shows that He is soon to die, wherefore also He made mention of a Testament, and He reminds them also of the former Testament, for that also was dedicated with blood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again He tells the cause of His death, “which is shed for many for the remission of sins;” and He saith, “Do this in remembrance of me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how He removes and draws them off from Jewish customs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For like as ye did that, He saith, in remembrance of the miracles in Egypt, so do this likewise in remembrance of me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That was shed for the preservation of the firstborn, this for the remission of the sins of the whole world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “This,” saith He, “is my blood, which is shed for the remission of sins.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But this He said, indicating thereby, that His passion and His cross are a mystery, by this too again comforting His disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And like as Moses saith, “This shall be to you for an everlasting memorial,”so He too, “in remembrance of me,” until I come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore also He saith, “With desire I have desired to eat this passover,”that is, to deliver you the new rites, and to give a passover, by which I am to make you spiritual.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He Himself drank of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For lest on hearing this, they should say, What then? do we drink blood, and eat flesh? and then be perplexed (for when He began to discourse concerning these things, even at the very sayings many were offended),therefore lest they should be troubled then likewise, He first did this Himself, leading them to the calm participation of the mysteries.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He Himself drank His own blood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then must we observe that other ancient rite also? some one may say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By no means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For on this account He said, “Do this,” that He might withdraw them from the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if this worketh remission of sins, as it surely doth work it, the other is now superfluous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As then in the case of the Jews, so here also He hath bound up the memorial of the benefit with the mystery, by this again stopping the mouths of heretics.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when they say, Whence is it manifest that Christ was sacrificed? together with the other arguments we stop their mouths from the mysteries also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if Jesus did not die, of what are the rites the symbols?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how much diligence hath been used, that it should be ever borne in mind that He died for us?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since the Marcionists, and Valentinians, and Manichæans were to arise, denying this dispensation, He continually reminds us of the passion even by the mysteries, (so that no man should be deceived); at once saving, and at the same time teaching by means of that sacred table.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this is the chief of the blessings; wherefore Paul also is in every way pressing this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, when He had delivered it, He saith, “I will not drink of the fruit of this wine, until that day when I drink it new with you in my Father’s kingdom.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For because He had discoursed with them concerning passion and cross, He again introduces what He has to say of His resurrection, having made mention of a kingdom before them,table, and rise up in drunkenness, whereas it were meet to give thanks, and end with an hymn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear this, as many as wait not again for the last prayer of the mysteries, for this is a symbol of that.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He gave thanks before He gave it to His disciples, that we also may give thanks.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He gave thanks, and sang an hymn after the giving, that we also may do this selfsame thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But for what reason doth He go forth unto the mountain?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Making Himself manifest, that He may be taken, in order not to seem to hide himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He hastened to go to the place which was also known to Judas.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then “He saith unto them, All ye shall be offended in me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this He mentions also a prophecy, “For it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep shall be scattered abroad:”at once persuading them ever to give heed to the things that are written, and at same time making it plain that He was crucified, according to God’s purpose; and by everything showing He was no alien from the old covenant, nor from the God preached therein, but that what is done is a dispensation,and that the prophets all proclaimed all things beforehand from the beginning that are comprised in the matter, so that they be quite confident about the better things also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He teaches us to know what the disciples were before the crucifixion, what after the crucifixion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed they who, when He was crucified, were not able so much as to stand their ground, these after His death were mighty, and stronger than adamant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this self-same thing is a demonstration of His death, the fright and cowardice, I mean, of His disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if when so many things have been both done and said, still some are shameless, and say that He was not crucified; if none of these things had come to pass, to what pitch of wickedness would they not have proceeded?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So for this reason, not by His own sufferings only, but by what took place with respect to the disciples, He confirms the word concerning His death, and by the mysteries also, in every way confounding those that are diseased with the pest of Marcion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this reason He suffers even the chief apostle to deny Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if He was not bound nor crucified, whence sprung the fear to Peter, and to the rest of the apostles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He suffers them not however, on the other hand, to wait until the sorrows, but what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But after I am risen again, I will go before you into Galilee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not from Heaven doth He appear at once, neither will He depart into any distant country, but in the same nation, in which He had also been crucified, nearly in the same place, so as hereby again to assure them that He that was crucified was the very same that rose again, and in this way to comfort them more abundantly when in sorrow.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore also He said “in Galilee,” that being freed from the fears of the Jews they might believe His saying.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For which cause indeed He appeared there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But Peter answered and said, Though all men should be offended because of Thee, yet will I never be offended.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What sayest thou, O Peter? the prophet said, “The sheep shall be scattered;” Christ hath confirmed the saying, and sayest thou, No?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Is not what passed before enough, when Thou saidst, “Far be it from Thee,”and thy mouth was stopped?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this then He suffers him to fall, teaching him thereby to believe Christ in all things, and to account His declaration more trustworthy than one’s own conscience.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the rest too reaped no small benefit from his denial, having come to know man’s weakness, and God’ s truth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when He foretells anything, we must no longer be subtle, nor lift up ourselves above the common sort.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “thy rejoicing,” it is said, “thou shalt have in thyself, and not in another.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For where he should have prayed, and have said, Help us, that we be not cut off, he is confident in himself, and saith, “Though all men should be offended in Thee, yet will I never;” though all should undergo this, I shall not undergo it, which led him on by little and little to self-confidence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Christ then, out of a desire to put down this, permitted his denial.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since he neither submitted to Him nor the prophet (and yet for this intent He brought in the prophet besides, that they may not gainsay), but nevertheless since he submitted not to His words, he is instructed by deeds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in proof that for this intent He permitted it, that He might amend this in him, hear what He saith, “I have prayed for thee, that thy faith fail not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this He said sharply reproving him, and showing that his fall was more grievous than the rest, and needed more help.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the matters of blame were two; both that he gainsaid; and, that he set himself before the other; or rather a third too, namely, that he attributed all to himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To cure these things then, He suffered the fall to take place, and for this cause also leaves the others, and addresses Himself earnestly to him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “Simon,”saith He, “Simon, behold Satan hath desired to have you that he may sift you as wheat;” that is, that he may trouble, confound, tempt you; but “I have prayed for thee, that thy faith fail not.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why, if Satan desired all, did He not say concerning all, I have prayed for you?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Is it not quite plain that it is this, which I have mentioned before, that it is as reproving him, and showing that his fall was more grievous than the rest, that He directs His words to him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And wherefore said He not, But I did not suffer it, rather than, “I have prayed?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He speaks from this time lowly things, on His way to His passion, that He may show His humanity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He that has built His church upon Peter’s confession, and has so fortified it, that ten thousand dangers and deaths are not to prevail over it; He that hath given him the keys of Heaven, and hath put him in possession of so much authority, and in no manner needed a prayer for these ends (for neither did He say, I have prayed, but with His own authority, “I will build my church, and I will give thee the keys of Heaven”), how should He need to pray, that He might brace up the shaken soul of a single man?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore then did He speak in this way?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the cause which I mentioned, and because of their weakness, for they had not as yet the becoming view of Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then was it that He denied? he said not, that thou mayest not deny, but that thy faith fail not, that thou perish not utterly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this came from His care.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed fear had driven out all else, for it was beyond measure, and it became beyond measure, since God had to an exceeding degree deprived him of His help, and He did exceedingly deprive him thereof, because there was to an exceeding degree in him the passion of self-will and contradiction.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In order then that He might pluck it up by the roots, therefore He suffered the terror to overtake him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in proof that this passion was grievous in him, he was not content with his former words, gainsaying both prophet and Christ, but also after these things when Christ had said unto him, “Verily I say unto thee, that this night,before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice,” he replieth, “Though I should die with Thee, I will not deny Thee in any wise.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Luke signifies moreover, that the more Christ warned him, so much the more did Peter exceedingly oppose Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What mean these things, O Peter?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When He was saying, “One of you shall betray me,” thou didst fear lest thou shouldest be the traitor, and didst constrain the disciple to ask, although conscious to thyself of no such thing; but now, when He is plainly crying out, and saying, “All shall be offended,” art thou gainsaying it, and not once only, but twice and often?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this is what Luke saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence then did this come to him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: From much love, from much pleasure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I mean, that after that he was delivered from that distressing fear about the betrayal, and knew the traitor, he then spoke confidently, and lifted himself up over the rest, saying, “Though all men shall be offended, yet will I not be offended.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And in some degree too his conduct sprung from jealousy, for at supper they reasoned “which of them is the greater,”to such a degree did this passion trouble them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He checked him, not compelling him to the denial, God forbid! but leaving him destitute of His help, and convicting human nature.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See at any rate after these things how he was subdued.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For after the resurrection, when he had said, “And what shall this man do?”and was silenced, he ventured no more to gainsay as here, but held his peace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again, towards the assumption,when he heard, “It is not for you to know times or seasons,”again he holds his peace, and contradicts not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After these things, on the house, and by the sheet, when he heard a voice saying to him, “What God hath cleansed, call not thou common,”even though he knew not for the time what the saying could be, he is quiet, and strives not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: All these things did that fall effect, and whereas before that he attributes all to himself, saying, “Though all men shall be offended, yet will I not be offended;” and, “If I should die, I will not deny Thee” (when he should have said, If I receive the assistance from Thee);—yet after these things altogether the contrary, “Why do ye give heed to us, as though by our own power or holiness we had made him to walk?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hence we learn a great doctrine, that a man’s willingness is not sufficient, unless any one receive the succor from above; and that again we shall gain nothing by the succor from above, if there be not a willingness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And both these things do Judas and Peter show; for the one, though he had received much help, was profited nothing, because he was not willing, neither contributed his part; but this one, though he was ready in mind, because he received no assistance, fell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed of these two things is virtue’s web woven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore I entreat you neither (when you have cast all upon God) to sleep yourselves, nor, when laboring earnestly, to think to accomplish all by your own toils.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither is it God’s will that we should be supine ourselves, therefore He worketh it not all Himself; nor yet boasters, therefore He did not give all to us; but having removed what was hurtful in either way, left that which is useful for us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He suffered even the chief apostle to fall, both rendering him more humbled in mind, and training him thenceforth to greater love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For to whom more is forgiven,” it is said, “he loveth more.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us then in everything believe God, and gainsay Him in nothing, though what is said seem to be contrary to our thoughts and senses, but let His word be of higher authority than both reasonings and sight.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus let us do in the mysteries also, not looking at the things set before us, but keeping in mind His sayings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For His word cannot deceive, but our senses are easily beguiled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That hath never failed, but this in most things goeth wrong.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since then the word saith, “This is my body,” let us both be persuaded and believe, and look at it with the eyes of the mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For Christ hath given nothing sensible, but though in things sensible yet all to be perceived by the mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So also in baptism, the gift is bestowed by a sensible thing, that is, by water; but that which is done is perceived by the mind, the birth, I mean, and the renewal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if thou hadst been incorporeal, He would have delivered thee the incorporeal gifts bare; but because the soul hath been locked up in a body, He delivers thee the things that the mind perceives, in things sensible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How many now say, I would wish to see His form, the mark, His clothes, His shoes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Lo! thou seest Him, Thou touchest Him, thou eatest Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And thou indeed desirest to see His clothes, but He giveth Himself to thee not to see only, but also to touch and eat and receive within thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let then no one approach it with indifference, no one faint-hearted, but all with burning hearts, all fervent, all aroused.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if Jews standing, and having on their shoes and their staves in their hands, ate with haste, much more oughtest thou to be watchful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they indeed were to go forth to Palestine, wherefore also they had the garb of pilgrims, but thou art about to remove unto Heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore it is needful in all respects to be vigilant, for indeed no small punishment is appointed to them that partake unworthily.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Consider how indignant thou art against the traitor, against them that crucified Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Look therefore, lest thou also thyself become guilty of the body and blood of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They slaughtered the all-holy body, but thou receivest it in a filthy soul after such great benefits.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither was it enough for Him to be made man, to be smitten and slaughtered, but He also commingleth Himself with us, and not by faith only, but also in very deed maketh us His body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then ought not he to exceed in purity that hath the benefit of this sacrifice, than what sunbeam should not that hand be more pure which is to sever this flesh, the mouth that is filled with spiritual fire, the tongue that is reddened by that most awful blood?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Consider with what sort of honor thou wast honored, of what sort of table thou art partaking.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That which when angels behold, they tremble, and dare not so much as look up at it without awe on account of the brightness that cometh thence, with this we are fed, with this we are commingled, and we are made one body and one flesh with Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Who shall declare the mighty works of the Lord, and cause all His praises to be heard?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What shepherd feeds his sheep with his own limbs?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why do I say, shepherd?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There are often mothers that after the travail of birth send out their children to other women as nurses; but He endureth not to do this, but Himself feeds us with His own blood, and by all means entwines us with Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Mark it, He was born of our substance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But, you say, this is nothing to all men; though it does concern all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if He came unto our nature, it is quite plain that it was to all; but if to all, then to each one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how was it, you say, that all did not reap the profit therefrom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This was not of His doing, whose choice it was to do this in behalf of all, but the fault of them that were not willing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With each one of the faithful doth He mingle Himself in the mysteries, and whom He begat, He nourishes by Himself, and putteth not out to another; by this also persuading thee again, that He had taken thy flesh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us not then be remiss, having been counted worthy of so much both of love and honor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See ye not the infants with how much eagerness they lay hold of the breast? with what earnest desire they fix their lips upon the nipple?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With the like let us also approach this table, and the nipple of the spiritual cup.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Or rather, with much more eagerness let us, as infants at the breast, draw out the grace of the spirit, let it be our one sorrow, not to partake of this food.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The works set before us are not of man’s power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He that then did these things at that supper, this same now also works them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: We occupy the place of servants.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He who sanctifieth and changeth them is the same.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let then no Judas be present, no covetous man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If any one be not a disciple, let him withdraw, the table receives not such.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “I keep the passover,” He saith, “with my disciples.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This table is the same as that, and hath nothing less.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is not so that Christ wrought that, and man this, but He doth this too.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This is that upper chamber, where they were then; and hence they went forth unto the mount of Olives.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us also go out unto the hands of the poor, for this spot is the mount of Olives.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the multitude of the poor are olive-trees planted in the house of God, dropping the oil, which is profitable for us there, which the five virgins had, and the others that had not received perished thereby.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having received this, let us enter in that with bright lamps we may meet the bridegroom; having received this, let us go forth hence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let no inhuman person be present, no one that is cruel and merciless, no one at all that is unclean.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These things I say to you that receive, and to you that minister.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is necessary to address myself to you also, that you may with much care distribute the gifts there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There is no small punishment for you, if being conscious of any wickedness in any man, you allow him to partake of this table.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “His blood shall be required at your hands.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Though any one be a general, though a deputy, though it be he himself who is invested with the diadem, and come unworthily, forbid him, the authority thou hast is greater than his.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thou, if thou wert entrusted to keep a spring of water clean for a flock, and then wert to see a sheep having much mire on its mouth, thou wouldest not suffer it to stoop down unto it and foul the stream: but now being entrusted with a spring not of water, but of blood and of spirit, if thou seest any having on them sin, which is more grievous than earth and mire, coming unto it, art thou not displeased? dost thou not drive them off? and what excuse canst thou have?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this end God hath honored you with this honor, that ye should discern these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This is your office, this your safety, this your whole crown, not that ye should go about clothed in a white and shining vestment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And whence know I, you may say, this person, and that person?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I speak not of the unknown, but of the notorious.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Shall I say something more fearful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is not so grievous a thing for the energumensto be within, as for such as these, whom Paul affirms to trample Christ under foot, and to “account the blood of the covenant unclean, and to do despite to the grace of the Spirit.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he that hath fallen into sin and draws nigh, is worse than one possessed with a devil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they, because they are possessed are not punished, but those, when they draw nigh unworthily, are delivered over to undying punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us not therefore drive away these only, but all without exception, whomsoever we may see coming unworthily.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let no one communicate who is not of the disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let no Judas receive, lest he suffer the fate of Judas.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This multitude also is Christ’s body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Take heed, therefore, thou that ministerest at the mysteries, lest thou provoke the Lord, not purging this body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Give not a sword instead of meat.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, though it be from ignorance that he come to communicate, forbid him, be not afraid.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Fear God, not man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If thou shouldest fear man, thou wilt be laughed to scorn even by him, but if God, thou wilt be an object of respect even to men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou darest not to do it thyself, bring him to me; I will not allow any to dare do these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I would give up my life rather than impart of the Lord’s blood to the unworthy; and will shed my own blood rather than impart of such awful blood contrary to what is meet.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if any hath not known the bad man, after much inquiry, it is no blame.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For these things have been said about the open sinners.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if we amend these, God will speedily discover to us the unknown also; but if we let these alone, wherefore should He then make manifest those that are hidden.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But these things I say, not that we repel them only, nor cut them off, but in order that we may amend them, and bring them back, that we may take care of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thus shall we both have God propitious, and shall find many to receive worthily; and for our own diligence, and for our care for others, receive great reward; unto which God grant we may all attain by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory world without end.
